response:
Nurse Emily organizes a creative writing workshop for the older adults in her care facility, ensuring that each participant has the opportunity to engage in a meaningful activity. She divides the 24 residents into groups of 6, resulting in 4 groups in total. To provide a structured session, Emily prepares 3 writing prompts per group, totaling 12 prompts for the initial session. 

To accommodate the possibility that groups may want to continue writing beyond the initial set, Emily has also prepared an additional set of 10 extra prompts. These are to be shared equally among all the groups. The library at the facility serves as a resource for the residents, offering a quiet space for reflection and creativity. Emily ensures that the prompts are varied and engaging, encouraging the residents to explore their thoughts and experiences. The library also houses a collection of books that inspire the writing process, further enriching the workshop experience. 

In total, Emily needs to have 12 initial prompts plus 10 extra prompts, resulting in 22 writing prompts prepared for the session. The library plays a vital role in supporting the residents' creative journey. The END.
